content.problemStatement:
You are given integers NNN and KKK.

Find a permutation PPP of length NNN such that:


‚àëi=1N(max‚Å°j=1iPj‚àímin‚Å°j=1iPj)=K\sum_{i=1}^{N} ( \max\limits_{j = 1}^{i} P_j - \min\limits_{j = 1}^{i} P_j ) = K‚àëi=1N‚Äã(j=1maxi‚ÄãPj‚Äã‚àíj=1mini‚ÄãPj‚Äã)=K
If there exists multiple PPP, you can print any one.

Otherwise report that it is not possible.

Note that a permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers NNN and KKK ‚àí-‚àí the length of PPP and the required sum.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if there does not exist any such PPP.
Otherwise, print NNN distinct positive integers, representing permutation PPP.


If there exists multiple PPP, you can print any one.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For the permutation [1][1][1], (max‚Å°j=11Pj‚àímin‚Å°j=11Pj)=1‚àí1=0(\max\limits_{j = 1}^{1} P_j - \min\limits_{j = 1}^{1} P_j) = 1-1 = 0(j=1max1‚ÄãPj‚Äã‚àíj=1min1‚ÄãPj‚Äã)=1‚àí1=0.
Test case 222: The only permutation of length 111 gives the sum 000.
Test case 333: It can be shown that no permutation of length 222 gives the sum 000.
Test case 444: Consider the permutation [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2].
The sum of difference between maximum and minimum in each prefix is (1‚àí1)+(3‚àí1)+(3‚àí1)=4(1-1) + (3-1) + (3-1) = 4(1‚àí1)+(3‚àí1)+(3‚àí1)=4.
